behaviors: Guarding the sarcophagus. Attack on sight.
Animated by old necromancy, motionless until disturbed.
Patrols a fixed path, ignores anything not threatening.
Shambling in place, waiting for the living.
Clustered around a body. Rise when approached within 10 ft.
Wedged in a coffin, falls out when opened.
Feasting on a corpse. Looks up when the door opens.
Lurking in a pit beneath the floor (Search to notice).
Masquerading as a fresh corpse on a slab.
Armed with corroded swords, standing at attention.
Collapsed in a pile — animates when treasure is touched.
Standing in alcoves along the walls. Animate on entry.
Risen grave-diggers, still holding rusted shovels.
Hiding behind a sarcophagus. Waits for the party to split.
Three ghouls sharing a meal. They do not like to be interrupted.
Pack behavior — one distracts, others flank.
Nesting in the burial niches.
Merges with natural shadows. Difficult to see until it attacks.
Drawn to light sources. Extinguishes torches as a tactic.
The former crypt-keeper, risen to guard the vault.
Seated on a stone throne, motionless until approached.
Drifting near the ceiling, invisible from below.
Bound to a specific object in the room.
Clustered in an unlit alcove.
Following the party since the last room.
Wight lord with 1d4 skeleton thralls.
Waiting in a sealed sarcophagus.
Ancient guardian, patient and malevolent.
Pack of wraiths circling the room's centerpiece.
The tomb's true occupant, risen in wrath.
Seated in repose, surrounded by offerings.
The high priest of the crypt, never truly dead.
Bound to protect the inner vault.
The dynasty's last ruler, wrapped in gold-thread linen.
Accompanied by 1d4 skeleton honor guard.
Guarding the approach to a mummy's tomb.
Former noble, unrecognizable now.
Ancient vampire resting in a sealed sarcophagus.
In bat form, roosting on the ceiling.
Playing dice. Scramble for weapons when alarmed.
On guard duty, bored and inattentive.
Arguing noisily. Heard from outside the room.
Sleeping in shifts. Half are awake.
Eating loudly around a fire pit.
Wolf-riders on patrol. The wolves smell the party first.
Handler and wolves blocking a chokepoint.
Squad leader drilling three goblins.
Enforcer collecting tribute from goblin quarters.
Wolves in a crude kennel, chained. They can be loosed.
Wolves roaming freely, loyal to a nearby goblin.
Hobgoblin war-band, well-equipped.
Mercenaries hired by the goblin chief.
Hobgoblin garrison, organized and alert.
Drilling formation — ready for a fight.
Hulking bugbears waiting in ambush. They are very patient.
Bugbear chief and bodyguards, seated and expecting tribute.
Gnoll raiders using this as a base.
Gnoll slavers with 1d3 captured prisoners.
Single massive bugbear, blocking the only exit.
Bugbear and two trained wolves.
Gnolls gambling over humanoid bones.
Gnoll patrol, just returned from outside.
Ogre mercenary the goblins are terrified of.
Ogre eating — interrupted meals make ogres angry.
Ogre warband allied with the goblin king.
Ogres with a shared treasure hoard.
Troll enforcer for the goblin king.
Troll sleeping in a pile of bones.
Ogre gang with crude ranged weapons (javelins).
Pair of trolls, hunting together.
Troll with a pack of wolves.
Hill giant warlord commanding a goblin army.
Hill giant prisoner the goblins foolishly tried to contain.
Three trolls in a feeding frenzy.
Cultists in mid-ritual. They do not take interruptions well.
Zealots guarding the inner sanctum.
Beetles nesting behind the altar, skitter out when disturbed.
Beetles attracted to old offerings on the floor.
Centipedes in the altar cracks. Hard to see.
Nesting under loose flagstones.
Cultist guard with a prisoner.
Cultists performing a summoning. Almost done.
Spiders in webbing on the ceiling. Drop on intruders.
Web-wrapped bodies along the wall conceal spiders.
Acolytes in prayer. Their god is listening.
Acolyte with a religious relic they will die to protect.
Medusa priestess of the old faith, still worshipped.
Medusa posing as a statue. Many real statues nearby for camouflage.
Veteran fanatics, trained fighters. Morale 10 near their altar.
Execution squad with a captured adventurer.
Gargoyles disguised as decorative carvings on pillars.
Temple guardians, bound to attack on blasphemy.
Giant spiders in elaborate webs across the ritual chamber.
Spider with a web-wrapped treasure chest.
Gargoyle perched above the entrance, watching.
High priest — an ogre mage in disguise as a holy elder.
Ogre mage conducting a rite of power.
Avatar of the temple's patron, finally manifest.
Lamia poses as the oracle the cultists serve.
Lamia surrounded by charmed cultist bodyguards.
Temple guardian, locked in the inner sanctum.
Basilisk with petrified adventurers as decoration.
Vampire cult leader worshipped as a dark deity.
Vampire posing as the temple's high priest.
Ancient lamia, the temple's true ruler.
Basilisk pair nesting in the sanctum.
Guards at ease. Challenging anyone who enters.
Bandits gambling. Weapons within reach.
Patrol, recently returned, still alert.
Off-duty bandits eating. Only half are armed.
Sleeping quarters — most are asleep.
Archers positioned on high ground in the room.
Marksmen covering the door.
Veteran sergeants running drills.
Experienced fighters guarding the captain's quarters.
Large bandit mob, rowdy and overconfident.
Bandits dividing loot from a recent raid.
The bandit captain, flanked by two veteran bodyguards.
Captain planning a raid on a map table.
Elite bandit core, professional mercenaries.
Veterans interrogating a prisoner.
Mercenaries hired to reinforce the lair.
Mercenary squad with a designated leader.
Orc mercenaries employed by the bandits.
Orcs and bandits sharing a tense meal.
Captain with full bodyguard retinue.
Lieutenant captain left in charge.
Gnoll auxiliaries, muscle for the bandits.
Gnolls doing the dirty work no bandit will.
Ogre enforcer for the bandit lord.
Ogre gatekeeper, very territorial.
Ogre war-band in the bandit army.
The bandit lord in their throne room.
Bandit lord with a full council of captains.
Troll prison guards. Their lair smells accordingly.
Troll bound in service to the bandit lord.
Bandit lord planning a major assault.
Elite mercenary company, professional and dangerous.
Battle-hardened veterans with magic equipment.
Ogre mob, recently recruited.
Bats roosting on the ceiling. Disturbed by light.
Colony disturbed by the party's noise.
Rats in a den, defending their nest.
Rats swarming a food cache they found.
Cave locusts filling the chamber. They shriek when startled.
Locusts grazing on fungal patches.
Mother bear with 1d2 cubs. Extremely aggressive.
Bear defending a food cache.
Ferrets hunting for prey. Fast and relentless.
Giant ferret with a nest of pups.
Gray ooze blending with wet cavern walls. Almost invisible.
Ooze covering the floor of a passage.
Cave bear in its den. Has it heard you yet?
Enormous cave bear with two half-grown cubs.
Ochre jelly oozing from a crack in the wall.
Jelly filling a low passage; unavoidable.
Wererats posing as human survivors.
Wererat thieves using the cave as a hideout.
Ghouls driven from a crypt, now hunting in the caves.
Cockatrice nesting among petrified victims.
Cockatrice flock blocking a passage.
Green slime hanging from the cave ceiling.
Slime covering an otherwise appealing ledge.
Cave bear pack — unheard of, but here we are.
Trolls using this cave as their hunting ground.
Minotaur lord of this cave system.
Minotaur guarding its territory with fury.
Purple worm burrowed up through the floor.
The sound of the worm approaching — roll init.
Troll feeding on a giant bat.
Young cave dragon claiming this as its lair.
Dragon sleeping on a small hoard.
Troglodytes that moved in after the dwarves fled.
Troglodyte hunting party, surprised to see you.
Kobold scavengers stripping dwarven metal.
Kobolds with a crude trap they're very proud of.
Mad dwarf survivors, deeply suspicious of outsiders.
Dwarf recluse who never left. Attacks on sight.
Dwarven ancestor statues, still animated by old magic.
Stone sentinel activates when treasure is approached.
Large troglodyte warband raiding the ruin.
Gnome treasure-hunters, not friendly to competition.
Gnome tinkerers operating a surviving dwarven machine.
Iron dwarf construct, guardian of the vault.
Iron sentinel awakening as the party enters.
Doppelganger posing as a friendly dwarven ghost.
Doppelgangers that have taken the forms of an earlier party.
Medusa using the ruin as a lair, surrounded by stone victims.
Dwarven berserkers, cursed to guard the ruin forever.
Mad dwarf warriors, attacking everything.
Stone gargoyles, mistaken for dwarven carvings.
Doppelganger has taken the form of a party member.
Dwarven stone golem, the final guardian of the vault.
Golem damaged by age — but still terrifying.
The legendary iron golem of Clan Deepdelve, still active.
Iron golem forged to protect the clan vault.
Trolls drawn by old rumors of dwarven gold.
Vampire lord who conquered the ruin centuries ago.
Second golem, paired with the first.
Young earth dragon denning in the collapsed great hall.
Apprentices working on an experiment. Interrupted.
Apprentice left in charge; panicking.
Escaped test subjects, extremely dangerous.
Spiders nesting among discarded spell components.
The wizard's little spies, defending their creator's lab.
Homunculi with orders to kill intruders.
Crystal guardian placed at the threshold.
Appears as decorative art. Activates when touched.
Resident mage, mid-experiment. Furious at the interruption.
Mage with a hostage using as a research subject.
The wizard's imp servants, chaotic and mischievous.
Imps escaped from a summoning circle.
Senior mage, powerful and paranoid.
Mage with a golem bodyguard.
Magically bound guardians, cannot leave the room.
Gargoyles sculpted to look like failed experiments.
The wizard's failed creation, dangerously unstable.
Flesh golem escaped from its containment.
Experiment gone wrong — basilisk in a shattered cage.
Basilisk with mirror-system that turns its gaze back on intruders.
Phase spiders from a botched summoning.
Phase spider hunting prey, appearing and vanishing.
Djinni bound in a flask. Released — and furious.
Bound djinni, neutral, will negotiate if spoken to.
The tower's master, expecting visitors.
Archmage mid-ritual, vulnerable only now.
Vampire archmage who betrayed the tower's founder.
Undead mage still conducting research.
A pair of flesh golems, the tower's last defense.
Efreeti imprisoned here. It will offer three wishes — with strings.
Efreeti freed from a shattered binding circle. Angry.
Two djinn, one bound and one free — they argue.
The tower's founder, never truly gone.
Lich in phylactery-meditation. Disturbing it is a mistake.
Squatting in the shallows. Tongue strikes before anyone notices it moved.
Roosting on a ledge above the water. Drops on the first person through.
Two toads sharing a narrow channel. They don't move for anything.
Swarm surging out of a cracked pipe. They're not afraid of you.
Rats gnawing on a bloated carcass. They scatter but circle back.
Nesting behind a collapsed grate. The nest has something in it.
Nearly invisible in the murk. You might walk past it.
Floating motionless in the center of a flooded chamber. Waiting.
Draped half-in, half-out of the water along the walkway.
Scavengers stripping a dead thing. They look up when the door opens.
Standing waist-deep in the outflow, watching the tunnel entrance.
A pack moving in loose formation, driven here from somewhere worse.
Clinging to the underside of a wooden walkway. Drops when weight crosses.
Barely distinguishable from the black slick on the walls.
Floating in a cistern. Looks like a waterlogged rope.
Coating the floor of a low passage. Unavoidable.
Blending perfectly with the wet stone wall. 90% chance to be missed.
Seeping in through a crack overhead. Still arriving.
Filling a flooded chamber — there is barely room for anything else.
Lurking beneath a collapsed walkway. The water here is very still.
Ancient and huge. Has been here longer than anyone remembers.
Territorial. The smell alone announces them thirty feet early.
Guarding a cached stash of salvage. They built it, they'll die for it.
Three brutes in a feeding dispute. They turn on intruders the moment they agree.
Posing as desperate sewer workers. The story almost holds.
Wererats using the lower cisterns as a base, watching from the dark.
One in human form negotiating; others have already flanked the party.
Occupying a whole side-passage. Something good is on the other side.
Oozing down a drain from above as the party moves beneath.
Disguised as spilled alchemical sludge on the floor.
Hanging from the ceiling of a low tunnel. Drip. Drip.
Covering the ladder rungs. Discovered too late.
Growing over a sealed door — only visible when you're already touching it.
Following the party through the pipes. The oozing sound is getting closer.
Filling a side-chamber completely. The chest in the middle is visible through it.
Oozing from a cracked cistern wall. Growing larger as it feeds.
Massive. Has been here for decades. Everything metal it touches corrodes.
Blocking the only passage forward. It is patient and it is not hungry.
A breeding pair. The female is between the party and the exit.
Patriarch of the lower cistern. Every mutoid down here defers to it.
A troll-thing warped by decades of alchemical runoff. Regenerates slime.
Squatting over a heap of gnawed bones. Looks up with milky, luminous eyes.
A war-pack under a hulking chieftain. They know these tunnels intimately.
Returning from a surface raid. Carrying stolen food and a captive.
Split into three smaller puddings from a past lightning strike. All hungry.
A colony — the whole flooded corridor is alive with them.
Leech nest in a collapsed tank. Disturbing the tank releases all of them.
Standing at attention in alcoves, as they have for centuries.
Collapsed in dust-drifts — they rise when the threshold is crossed.
Scattered across the floor; they reassemble at the sound of voices.
Their glow is the only light down here. They're also territorial.
Beetles drawn to residual magical heat in the walls. Disturbed by light.
A colony nesting in a cracked relief sculpture. The glow is beautiful. Don't touch it.
Adapted to perfect stillness in dry dust. The pincers twitch.
Sheltering in the shadow of a toppled column. Barely visible.
One hunting, two more somewhere nearby.
Swarming out of a crack in a desiccated fresco.
Living inside a hollow pillar that sheds them when struck.
Concealed in the bone-dust on the floor. Moving looks like rippling.
Scavengers adapted to mineral-licking for hydration. Skittish but territorial.
Basking in the faint warmth of magical residue in the walls.
A clutch — small ones everywhere, large ones defending.
Filling a vaulted chamber. Their shriek echoes catastrophically.
Grazing on patches of pale lichen that glow where the old magic is strongest.
Dormant in a pile until something warm disturbs them.
Anchored in the ceiling of a passage, filaments hanging like tripwires.
A cave fisher has been here long enough that bones litter the floor below it.
Two perched at opposite ends of a chamber. Filaments cross in the middle.
Scavengers from the surface, deep and thirsty. They'll fight for water.
A war-band stripped down to essentials, moving fast, looking for something.
Gnolls with a gnawed prisoner they're debating the value of.
Guardians of the elder people, still at their post. Motionless until approached.
A sentinel half-buried in collapse. One arm is free. One is enough.
Positioned in front of a sealed vault door. It hasn't moved in two centuries.
The desiccated ruler of whatever this place was. Still enthroned.
Wrapped in scorched linen, surrounded by offerings turned to ash.
A mummy so dry it barely registers as organic until it moves.
Huge. Has grown large feeding on cave fishers and lizards.
Guarding a dead end where it has made its lair. The bones suggest it wins.
Ghouls driven down from higher crypts by something worse. Desperate.
Desiccated ghouls, dry as paper, but no less dangerous for it.
Two dynastic rulers, entombed together. They remember each other.
A mummy lord with 1d4 skeleton honor guard still at their stations.
An iron bull, wandering halls too large for anything else. Its breath leaves stone.
Gorgon guarding the deepest vault. The statues outside were adventurers.
The last guardian, built by the elder people, still faithfully waiting.
A golem damaged by time, missing one arm. Still an eight-HD problem.
Passing through the walls, pausing to investigate the party's metal equipment.
A xorn has been living in the stone here for generations. It is not pleased to see you.
Nesting in the deepest vault. The statues are not decorative.
A basilisk with a collection of former treasure-hunters arranged artfully.
Burrowed up through the floor of the largest chamber. The sound precedes it.
A purple worm that has colonized a whole wing. The tunnels are its burrows.
Oath-bound to this place. Challenges the party formally before attacking.
A lone knight in tarnished armor, carrying a quest they cannot complete alone.
Three knights of opposing courts, currently at impasse. The party is a new variable.
Enchanted wolves, sleek and silver-eyed. They understand Common.
Pack serving an absent fae lord. They test the party's worthiness.
Wolves that are not entirely wolves. They leave no tracks.
Knights who broke an oath and cannot die until it is fulfilled.
The former champions of this place, risen in service to old geasa.
Wights still wearing heraldic devices. The devices are recognizable.
Redcaps — fae-touched goblins with iron-shod boots and terrible morale against silver.
Boggart militia, nominally serving a fae court. Demanding a toll.
Hobgoblins bound to this hall by compact. They resent it. They resent you.
Knight-errants who have lost their patrons and turned to the old ways.
Wandering men-at-arms, desperate, pledged to no lord. They'll negotiate.
Sell-swords who stumbled in and now can't find the exit.
The curse-weavers of this hall. Their webs carry enchantments.
Spiders that look almost like crabs. No one knows why. The fae don't explain.
Nesting in a heap of armor that once belonged to someone important.
The wronged dead — knights murdered, lords betrayed. They are specific about their grievances.
Oath-wraiths, bound to repeat a moment of failure until someone ends it.
Wraiths circling a sealed chapel. They guard something and hate doing it.
Seated at a table. Makes an offer. The offer involves the party's necks.
Standing in the entrance of a green chapel, axe in hand. Very still.
The green knight is on a quest. The party has accidentally become part of it.
Heraldic guardians, bound to the coats of arms carved into the pillars.
Perched in the vaulted ceiling, mistaken for ornamental grotesques.
Fae-bound stone things. They speak riddles. They do not have to make sense.
The enchantress at the heart of this place, wearing the face of a holy woman.
A medusa posing as an oracle. The petrified figures around her were believers.
Medusa and her 1d6 charmed knight servants. She calls them her champions.
The shadows of knights who refused to leave. They are not the knights.
Shadows that move against the light source, following the party since two rooms ago.
Cast by nothing. Moving with intention through a hall lit by sourceless light.
A lord of the fae court, here on errand. The errand is not explained.
Two knights of opposing allegiance, bound by prophecy to meet here. The party is the witness.
The lady of this place, who died in it and never forgave the fact.
A banshee bound to warn of doom. She is overdue. She is also angry.
Lights leading deeper in, toward the sound of a horn that isn't there.
Three wisps — one real, two reflections. Only one is dangerous. Probably.
A will-o'-wisp playing at being the Holy Grail. It's worked before.
A cursed lord, immortal by compact with something older than the kingdom.
Seated at the head of a long table, waiting for a worthy enough guest.
The lady of the lake, considerably less helpful than the legends suggest.
A lamia who has been the oracle of this place for three centuries. Many questors have come. Few left.
The original — the compact made flesh. It is very old and very patient.
An elder green knight whose head has been carried so long it has opinions.
The entire former garrison, risen in service to the last oath sworn here.
A wight court, with its own hierarchy, its own grievances, and a wight king.
A raiding band, returned from the surface. They took something. It cries.
Gathered around a fire, performing something that might be ritual. They stop.
A pack in the dark — you hear them before you see anything. Then silence.
Unnaturally quiet for rats. Something else is with them. Something is using them.
Nest in the debris. They scatter. One doesn't.
Rats that have been here long enough to learn the party's route.
Colony in the vault above. Disturbed by torchlight. Not hostile — not yet.
A few bats following the party since three chambers back.
The bats are leaving. All of them. Quickly.
Disguised as human survivors of something. Their story has holes.
Wererats that have gone feral. They don't maintain human form anymore.
One in each shadow. They've been tracking the party since the entrance.
Everywhere underfoot. Unavoidable without light.
In the walls. The walls are moving. Not the walls.
Under the body in the corner. Disturbing it releases them.
In the dark, you hear them before you can see them.
Something has disturbed a colony nearby. The shrieking started a minute ago.
Cave locusts filling a passage. They spit at torchlight.
Something has been following the party. Not quite a wolf. Not quite a man.
A pack-leader and two turned followers. They hunt in relays.
Werewolf in human form, sitting by a cold fire. Friendly. For now.
You didn't see it enter the room. It was already here.
A crawler that has learned patience. It is better at waiting than the party is.
Pale and enormous. Something humanity drove underground before it had writing.
A shape in the dark that does not resolve into anything familiar.
The sound is wrong — too wet, too large, too close.
Something ancient and hateful, drawn by the warmth of torchlight.
Ghouls that have been here since the age of sacrifice. They remember the taste.
Dug up through the floor from an older layer. This was their place first.
A ghoul that looks almost like someone the party might know.
Its den. The bones at the entrance were people.
A huge old bear with something wrong about the eyes.
Two — a male and a female, separated by the party's path. They are not happy.
Raiders from a deeper tribe. Surface-bleached and desperate.
Orcs that have been underground long enough to change. Something about the eyes.
A war-band performing a ritual they don't understand over a body they do.
The great horror. Old before the people above built their first wall.
It has no eyes. It doesn't need them. It knows.
A pack of the fully-turned — they don't come back from it anymore.
An entire extended family, all turned. They moved in together. They hunt together.
Three bears, territory overlapping. They are not coordinated. That makes it worse.
A bear the size of a cart horse. Ancient. Wrong.
Something so old it predates the concept of vampire. It just is what it is.
A primal blood-drinker with no court, no pretension, and nothing to negotiate.
A troll under the oldest part of the dungeon. Night-black and nearly silent.
Two trolls sharing a territory, each blaming the other for the party's intrusion.
An elder pale crawler. The others fled when it moved in.
It was waiting in the deepest room. It has always been waiting.
Goblins working a legitimate toll operation. They have a charter. It is notarized.
Guild goblins on a contracted salvage run. Their paperwork is impeccable.
Goblins who insist they are the party's hirelings. No one hired them. They are very good at it.
A wizard's shopping list that became self-aware and is now aggressively helpful to the wrong people.
The wizard's messengers, carrying a note they will not let the party read.
Homunculi defending a laboratory. The laboratory is inside a chest.
A door. It has a face. It talks. It will only open for a correct password it refuses to reveal.
Dungeon furniture come alive in protest of recent renovations.
Two statues in an ongoing argument. Interrupting them is unwise.
The dungeon's official welcome party. They are robbing you. It is a tradition.
Bandits who got lost. They have been here three weeks. They have opinions about the layout.
Adventurers. From a different dungeon. They are also confused.
Imps escaped from a summoning circle. The circle was a doodle. They are offended.
Four imps running a betting pool on the party's survival. They are rooting for the monsters.
An imp that insists it is the dungeon's manager and the party is a liability.
Beetles wearing tiny saddles. The goblins are very proud of this.
Giant fire beetles organized in a perfect formation. Someone trained them.
A single enormous beetle that has developed opinions about treasure.
Gargoyles who moonlight as architectural critics. They have strong opinions about the stonework.
The dungeon's original décor. They have been here so long they've formed a neighborhood association.
A gargoyle that is also the quest-giver. It has been waiting here for sixty years.
Doppelgangers that have been copying each other for so long none of them remember the original.
A doppelganger that has taken the form of the dungeon itself (conceptually).
Four doppelgangers pretending to be the party. They are better at it than expected.
A mimic that has been the main door for eleven years. It is tired of this.
Currently a treasure chest. The treasure inside is also the mimic.
A mimic imitating the entrance to the boss room. The actual boss room is elsewhere.
A djinni bound in a lamp that the goblins have been using as a paperweight.
Free djinni, furious, granting exactly what was asked for in the worst possible interpretation.
Djinni who claims to be the dungeon's owner. The dungeon does not dispute this.
A medusa who runs a very successful art gallery. The medium is permanent.
Medusa in the role of quest-giver. The quest involves her ex-husband, who is a statue.
The medusa is also petrified. Reflexive gaze. Very awkward.
A flesh golem assembled incorrectly. It functions anyway, though not as designed.
The flesh golem that runs the dungeon's tavern. The ale is fine. Don't ask about the ingredients.
Extremely rude flesh golem. The wizard built it with a surplus of spite.
Two djinn disputing ownership of this room. They will accept the party as arbitrators.
A djinni who has been wishing for things on behalf of the dungeon for forty years.
The lich is the dungeon. All of it. It has opinions about the party's route choices.
A lich that has forgotten it's a lich. Identifies as the dungeon's caretaker. Is doing a fine job.
Efreeti doing exactly what was asked: the dungeon is on fire.
An efreeti running a legitimate wish-brokerage. The fine print is the problem.
The vampire archmage who designed the dungeon. Considers the party a playtester.
A vampire mage in mid-experiment. The experiment involves the party now.
The golem is a door. The door is the golem. Opening it requires defeating it.
Stone golem programmed with the wrong instructions. It is politely and inexorably doing the wrong thing.
The whole room is a mimic. This is technically possible. It has happened.
A mimic that has been the exit for three months. It has let people out. It always lets them back in.
Scavengers in hazmat scraps, checking the party for edible technology.
A work-gang maintaining something they don't understand. Protective of it.
Gamma mutants who have formed a religion around a broken vending machine.
Rats three generations into irradiation. Slightly too large. Slightly too smart.
A swarm living inside the machinery. Disturbing the machine disturbs the rats.
Giant rats that glow faintly. They have been eating the walls.
Security units still running a patrol loop from before the collapse.
A death machine that has decided the party is unauthorized personnel. It is correct.
Feral machines pursuing an optimization goal no one programmed into them.
Tribals with salvaged weapons and strong opinions about territory.
A community of station mutants who have built something approximating civilization.
Station mutants on a foraging run, nervous and well-armed.
A scouting party from a larger settlement deeper in. They report back.
Gamma mutants gone feral — the radiation has pushed cognition sideways.
Industrial waste that has become something else. It corrodes everything.
A gray ooze seeping from a ruptured tank. Still growing.
Apex mutants who have lived in the hot zones long enough to thrive there.
A brute serving as enforcer for a mutant faction. The faction is watching.
Three mutant brutes competing to be first through a narrow corridor. At the party's expense.
An escaped bioweapon that has been evolving in the lower levels for decades.
A bio-horror that has learned to use doors. Not helpful.
Something the scientists made to solve a problem. They were right about the solution. Wrong about everything else.
A maintenance unit whose goal-functions cascaded into something violent.
Last active security chassis in this sector. It is very committed to its original purpose.
A feral robot that has welded salvage onto itself. It is enormous. It is angry.
Toxic waste aggregate — organic, barely. It eats everything including the floor.
An ochre jelly that came up through the drainage system. It's bigger than the drain it came from.
Bioengineered phase spiders escaped from a containment unit. Phase capability was not intended.
Phase spiders hunting in shifts — while one is ethereal the others are not.
A phase spider using the walls as transit. You can hear it moving through them.
Viral lycanthropy strain. Wererats acting as a mutagenic cult.
Wererats who maintain human form because it's tactically useful. They're calculating.
Heavy chassis — designed for something industrial. Repurposed itself for war.
Two feral robots that have been fighting each other for years. The party is a truce opportunity.
The original. Everything else in this wing grew from it.
A bio-horror that has absorbed and incorporated mechanical components. Parts of it still function.
Industrial waste so concentrated it developed persistence. Corrodes everything it contacts.
A black pudding that has been eating the facility for years. The facility is noticeably smaller.
An army of mutant brutes organized under a barely-coherent warlord.
Mutant brutes that have adapted to the hot zones — radiation just makes them angrier.
A pre-collapse construction unit, still functional. Its build orders are catastrophically outdated.
The facility's original security golem. It has not received a shutdown command.
A genetically engineered wyrm escaped from the breeding program. The program's goals were unclear.
Young earth dragon that has been feeding on the mineral-rich walls. It's considerably larger than young.
Locals who have opinions about unauthorized access to their section.
A crew of station mutants maintaining something in exchange for territorial rights.
Station mutants who have been here so long they've forgotten what the station was for.
Crawlers phase-shifting in and out of the walls. The walls are not solid in this section.
One visible, others phased. They take turns.
A void crawler that has been riding the hull for years. It just came inside.
Grey outsiders from whatever experiment produced them. Still in scrubs.
A cell of outsiders with a collective goal they share telepathically. They won't say what it is.
Grey outsiders who believe the party is the experiment. They have clipboards.
Space rats — technically the same species, technically not. They adapted.
A rat swarm living in the conduit system. Disturbing the conduits disturbs the rats.
Rats that have learned to use the vents as escape routes. They are faster than expected.
Colonial bats in a decompressed section with enough atmosphere to sustain them, barely.
Bats that echolocate through walls. They know the party is coming.
The bats are leaving. All of them. From this whole section. Fast.
A patrol, nervous, well-armed, and unclear on the rules of engagement.
Station mutant scavengers who have a good thing going and don't want it complicated.
Maintenance constructs that reclassified everything as waste to be processed.
A rogue construct that has been repurposing other robots for years. It has an army.
Two constructs mid-disagreement about a process parameter. The party is a welcome distraction.
Space slugs that came aboard in cargo and have been a problem ever since.
A slug nest — the adults are outside, the young are inside everything valuable.
A space slug that has eaten through a load-bearing wall. The room is unstable.
An elder void crawler, phasing in and out with full control. It is hunting.
Two crawlers coordinating — one phases in, the other waits outside to intercept.
Phase spiders using the station's own infrastructure as hunting ground.
A phase spider that has laid eggs in the between-space. The eggs are starting to hatch.
Phase spiders and void crawlers sharing territory. Uneasily.
Alien mimics that copy form. They don't quite get the faces right.
Doppelgangers that have been copying crew members for years. The crew is mostly doppelgangers now.
A doppelganger that has copied the party leader. It copied them three minutes ago.
Something from the xenobiology section that should not have been kept in one section.
A bio-horror adapted to microgravity. It moves in ways that don't work in normal gravity.
Something that came in through the hull breach in section seven. It has been here since.
A stellar horror that has been communicating with crew members. The crew members are changed.
Elite chassis — the original security complement. Still running original directives.
A rogue construct that has been the de-facto commander of this section for years.
The station's original AI, stored in a phylactery-analogue. It considers itself the rightful owner.
A lich who boarded this station three hundred years ago and never left. It has made improvements.
A bound djinni the old crew used for life-support backup. The binding is fraying.
Free djinni, enormous, furious, and technically responsible for the current atmospheric situation.
An efreeti maintaining the reactor by sheer force of will and spite. It wants to leave. It cannot.
An efreeti that escaped its binding years ago. It's still here because it finds it amusing.
The station's original heavy security units, upgraded by decades of self-modification.
Two feral robots that have incorporated biological components. They are no longer entirely robots.
